title: Ontology Walkthrough
description: A guided tour of the ontology structure hidden inside the loaded RDF graph.

stops.title: Ontology Overview
stops.narrative: This view isolates every OWL/RDFS class that graph-browser auto-detected from the RDF source. The ontology nodes are generated directly from the triples, so there is no second hand-maintained graph model to keep in sync.

stops.title: Class Hierarchy
stops.narrative: Subclass edges reveal the taxonomy of the dataset. Root concepts sit at the shallowest depth, and deeper classes inherit structure as the hierarchy expands.

stops.title: Property Relationships
stops.narrative: Object properties connect classes through their declared domains and ranges. This turns the ontology from a pure tree into a navigable relational model.

stops.title: Alignment Edges
stops.narrative: Equivalent classes and equivalent properties expose where this ontology aligns with neighboring vocabularies. Those mappings are useful when comparing datasets or tracing interoperability boundaries.
